draft.title: a CRM for freelancers
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
a CRM for freelancers

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers want visual contrast between navigation and active work—dark framing creates focus, bright center reduces cognitive load
facade.axisTargeted: organic vs digital / calm vs energetic
facade.label: Dark sidebar, bright workspace
facade.acceptImplies: User favors structured, high-contrast interfaces that separate 'thinking' zones from 'doing' zones
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers unified color temperature or softer visual hierarchy—less compartmentalized feel

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers want high contrast between navigation and workspace for task focus
facade.label: Dark sidebar, light content
facade.acceptImplies: Probe darker UI schemes, high-contrast layouts, mode toggles
facade.rejectImplies: Explore unified tone palettes, softer contrast, single-color schemes

evidence.hypothesis: Freelancers want visual contrast between navigation and active work—dark framing creates focus, bright center reduces cognitive load
evidence.implication: User favors structured, high-contrast interfaces that separate 'thinking' zones from 'doing' zones

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers want all contact data visible at once in rows and columns rather than scrolling through individual cards
facade.label: Dense client table
facade.acceptImplies: User values scan-ability and comparison across multiple clients simultaneously; prefers spreadsheet-like layouts for CRM
facade.rejectImplies: User finds information overload in grids; prefers progressive disclosure and focused views of one or few clients at a time

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer a CRM that feels like talking to a friend rather than filing paperwork
facade.label: Casual and conversational
facade.acceptImplies: The voice should use contractions, humor, and everyday language; error messages and empty states should feel supportive not stern
facade.rejectImplies: The interface should adopt formal, corporate language; neutral tone; strict adherence to business jargon and traditional CRM conventions

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer animated panel transitions over instant state changes when managing client details, because gradual motion feels less jarring during rapid context-switching
facade.axisTargeted: Animated transitions vs instant UI state changes
facade.label: Smooth slide-in
facade.acceptImplies: Build with motion-forward interactions; panels, modals, and overlays should glide and fade rather than pop; transitions are part of the information hierarchy
facade.rejectImplies: Prioritize snappy, instant responses; every interaction should feel immediate and clickable; motion is secondary to perceived performance

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer a persistent navigation sidebar paired with card-based layouts for viewing clients, projects, and invoices—reducing cognitive load from tabbed navigation.
facade.axisTargeted: Navigation structure & content hierarchy (sidebar vs tabs) combined with content container format (cards vs lists)
facade.label: Sidebar with cards
facade.acceptImplies: User leans toward persistent, always-visible navigation; likely favors contained, scannable information units over dense lists or tab switching.
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer tab-based or top navigation; might favor compact, list-heavy layouts with more content density per view.

axes.label: Whether they prefer minimal, clean aesthetics vs. rich visual hierarchy with color/imagery—reveals if they want a distraction-free workspace or one that feels energetic and branded
Whether they favor dense information dashboards vs. progressive disclosure (show only what's needed now)—reveals workflow preference for freelancers juggling multiple clients
Whether they want a professional, formal tone vs. casual, encouraging tone—reveals if they see the tool as business-critical infrastructure or a supportive sidekick
Whether they prefer calm, focused atmosphere vs. energizing, motivational atmosphere—reveals emotional context they want while managing their business
Whether they want client/project data front-and-center vs. financial/invoice data prioritized—reveals what information matters most to their freelance workflow
Whether they prefer instant feedback and snappy interactions vs. smooth, considered animations—reveals if they value speed and directness or polish and delight

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they prefer minimal, clean aesthetics vs. rich visual hierarchy with color/imagery—reveals if they want a distraction-free workspace or one that feels energetic and branded
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they favor dense information dashboards vs. progressive disclosure (show only what's needed now)—reveals workflow preference for freelancers juggling multiple clients
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they want a professional, formal tone vs. casual, encouraging tone—reveals if they see the tool as business-critical infrastructure or a supportive sidekick
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they prefer calm, focused atmosphere vs. energizing, motivational atmosphere—reveals emotional context they want while managing their business
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they want client/project data front-and-center vs. financial/invoice data prioritized—reveals what information matters most to their freelance workflow
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they prefer instant feedback and snappy interactions vs. smooth, considered animations—reveals if they value speed and directness or polish and delight
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer stripped-down visual language that removes decorative elements—reducing interface noise helps them maintain mental energy across multiple client dashboards and contexts within a single session
facade.axisTargeted: Organic vs Digital (leaning toward digital minimalism)
facade.label: Minimal, monochrome
facade.acceptImplies: User gravitates toward interfaces that feel intentional and stripped back; they may avoid soft, natural, or heavily textured design patterns
facade.rejectImplies: User wants visual warmth, texture, or personality in the interface; they may find sparse design cold or impersonal for solo work environments
